implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-002 — both pass subcommand starting with 'graphiti ' so run()'s resolver-skip branch fires
AC-003 — no --context flags assembled (no extra_context_paths threaded, no '--context' in args)
AC-004 — JSON string returned on every code path; ADR-ARCH-025 envelope on internal exception; never raises
AC-005 — function bodies wrapped in try/except Exception as exc:
AC-006 — structured logging with tool_name/duration_ms/status (structlog or stdlib-shim)
AC-007 — 21 unit tests cover all four status paths plus the no-context invariant
AC-008 — black --check + ruff check both report zero errors on the two new files

completion_promises.criterion_text: Both wrappers in src/forge/tools/graphiti.py, each decorated with @tool(parse_docstring=True)
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/tools/graphiti.py exports guardkit_graphiti_add_context and guardkit_graphiti_query, each defined under @tool(parse_docstring=True). Verified at runtime: both are LangChain BaseTool instances; args_schema.model_fields contains doc_path/group/repo and query/group/repo respectively; the parsed description starts with 'Run `guardkit graphiti …`'. TestDecoratorSurface.* (4/4) passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Both call forge.adapters.guardkit.run() with a subcommand starting with 'graphiti ' so the resolver is not invoked
completion_promises.evidence: _invoke_graphiti() forwards literal subcommand='graphiti add-context' / 'graphiti query' into guardkit_run. Captured kwargs in TestSubcommandPrefixBypassesResolver.* assert subcommand exact-match AND .startswith('graphiti '), which satisfies run()'s _is_graphiti_subcommand head-token detector (TASK-GCI-008) and triggers the resolver-skip branch.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No --context flags appear in the assembled command line for either wrapper, even when a manifest exists in the target repo
completion_promises.evidence: _invoke_graphiti() deliberately does NOT accept or thread an extra_context_paths kwarg through to guardkit_run() (contrast with the GCI-009 helper which does). TestNoContextFlagInArgv.* (4 tests) assert '--context' not in kwargs['args'] AND kwargs.get('extra_context_paths') in (None, []) for both tools. Combined with AC-002 (resolver skipped at run() level), no --context token can appear in the final argv.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Both return a JSON string; on internal exception, return {"status":"error","error":"..."} per ADR-ARCH-025 — never raises
completion_promises.evidence: Success/failed/timeout paths return result.model_dump_json() (canonical GuardKitResult). Exception path uses _error_json() with json.dumps to build {'status':'error','error':'<ExcType>: <msg>','tool':'<name>'} — safe under quote/backslash payloads. TestReturnIsJsonString.* (3) and TestInternalExceptionsAreAbsorbed.* (3) verify all four code paths.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Function body wrapped in try/except Exception as exc:
completion_promises.evidence: Each @tool body is wrapped in try/except Exception as exc: returning _error_json(exc, tool_name). _invoke_graphiti() has its own try/except as defence-in-depth. TestInternalExceptionsAreAbsorbed.test_*_returns_error_json_when_run_raises confirms exceptions never propagate.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each wrapper logs via structlog with tool_name, duration_ms, status
completion_promises.evidence: Module-level logger built via _build_logger() prefers structlog.get_logger('forge.tools.graphiti') and falls back to a kwargs-accepting _StdlibStructLogger shim if structlog is unavailable. Success path calls logger.info('guardkit_graphiti_tool_invocation', tool_name=..., duration_ms=..., status=result.status); error path calls logger.exception(..., status='error'). TestStructuredLogging.* (2 tests) assert the contractual triple is captured on logger.info kwargs.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests: each wrapper returns the right JSON shape on success/failed/timeout/exception; verify (via fake run()) that no --context flag is added regardless of the target repo's manifest
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/tools/test_graphiti.py organises 21 tests by AC. Coverage: success/failed/timeout JSON shape (TestReturnIsJsonString), exception → ADR-ARCH-025 envelope (TestInternalExceptionsAreAbsorbed), no --context in kwargs (TestNoContextFlagInArgv), 'graphiti ' subcommand prefix (TestSubcommandPrefixBypassesResolver), parse_docstring schema (TestDecoratorSurface), kwargs structured-logging contract (TestStructuredLogging), AST-based resolver-import guard (TestModuleHygiene), and absolute-allowlist-eligible cwd (TestRepoConfinement). All 21 pass; the wider tests/forge/adapters/guardkit/ + tests/forge/tools/ regression set (242 tests) also passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Turn 2 fix: ran black --line-length 88 --target-version py312 (.claude/rules/code-style.md mandates 'Use Black formatter, Max line length: 88') against both new files and accepted the reformat (a few line-collapse changes in test_graphiti.py — no semantic change). Then ran ruff check, which flagged one F401 'unittest.mock imported but unused' in test_graphiti.py — fixed via ruff check --fix (the import was a leftover from an earlier draft). Final state: `black --check --line-length 88 --target-version py312 src/forge/tools/graphiti.py tests/forge/tools/test_graphiti.py` reports 'All done! ✨ 🍰 ✨ 2 files would be left unchanged.' and `ruff check src/forge/tools/graphiti.py tests/forge/tools/test_graphiti.py` reports 'All checks passed!'. All 21 tests still pass after the lint fixes (and the wider 242-test regression set is unchanged).
